prompt: I need to add OAuth2 login (Google and GitHub providers) to our existing Express.js REST API. We currently use session-based auth with Passport.js. The API serves a React frontend at app.example.com. Users table is in PostgreSQL with columns: id, email, password_hash, created_at. We need to keep existing email/password login working while adding the OAuth options. The React frontend already has login/signup pages — we just need the backend endpoints and the frontend buttons/callbacks. We use Jest for testing and deploy to AWS ECS via GitHub Actions.

assertions.text: Output contains a Decomposition Summary section with Goal, Affected Areas, Workstreams, Risk, and Execution Waves
Tasks are organized into execution waves with clear sequencing and dependency references
Every task card includes at minimum: Goal, In Scope, Non-Goals, Dependencies, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands
Each task card has a single primary purpose — no card combines unrelated concerns
Cards do not fabricate exact filenames or paths that were not provided in the prompt (may reference subsystems or estimated areas)
Acceptance criteria are observable/testable — no vague phrases like 'works correctly' or 'is production ready'
Database schema changes (adding OAuth columns to users table) are in a separate card from application logic
Output includes a Follow-up Tasks section (even if 'None')
